title: Regime-Specific Models For Wet, Testing, And Late Race
summary: Wet/intermediate tyres, pre-season testing, and final-lap phases likely obey different pit logic from normal dry race stints.
rationale: Wet/intermediate tyres, pre-season testing, and final-lap phases likely obey different pit logic from normal dry race stints. Existing solutions add wet/dry indicators but still train one global decision surface, which can dilute rare-regime ranking.
implementation_hint: Train specialist models or add meta-features from separate LightGBM/CatBoost models for dry race rows, wet/intermediate rows, Pre-Season Testing, and RaceProgress>0.85. Blend specialist predictions with the global model only for matching rows, using OOF predictions for the blend weight search.
expected_effect: May improve public score if rare regimes have enough test representation and global model currently miscalibrates their pit probabilities.
risk: Small subsets can overfit and hurt AUC if blended too aggressively; Pre-Season Testing may have non-race behavior that is hard to validate.
